different tenses from the original speaker (because we  are most often talking at a When  we  report what somebody said or thought, it is usually natural to use 1 past    reporting       verbs:    He  said     he   didn't      like    the    party.

259 indirect      speech:    t enses    ________________ She said (that) she'd had enough. I think (that) you're probably right.

(e.g. say, think) in informal speech. For more details, ►    265.

The  conjunction that is often dropped, especially after common reporting verbs 5 dropping       that the-parfy-.. . - when Daniel is talking, the party is finished.) not James said that he didn't like the party... (     James-saidr-that A  change of time may mean a change of tense.

They promised to be there the next day.

We'll be there tomorrow.

He said he'd had an accident the day before.

I had an accident yesterday.

He said Anna had got her licence the Tuesday before.

Anna got her licence last Tuesday.

She said she'd be back the next week, but I never saw her again.

I'll be back next week.

Some other 'here and now’  words: next, last, yesterday, tomorrow. Compare: not ... he wanted to go home, (     .. . to-go hom e now.) this party. ..) not James said that he didn't like the party... (     James-satd-thaf-kodidn^f-likt because he is no longer at the party.

this, now, today. Daniel, reporting what James said, does not use this and now A  change of place and time may mean changing or dropping words like here, 3 'here    and   now'   words James said that he didn't like                  ...) about what James said, naturally use he.

In the above example, James says I to refer to himself. Jackie and Daniel, talking A  change of speaker may mean a change of pronoun.

complicated rules about indirect speech in English.

These differences are mostly natural and logical, and it is not necessary to learn wanted to go home.

DANIEL   (on Sunday morning): James said that he didn't like the party, and he Noun Clauses, Direct and Indirect Speech    Section 24 grammar •  259  indirect speech: tenses For more details.  ►    263.3.

He said it would be nice if we could meet.

It would be nice if we could meet.

Past modal verbs are usually unchanged in indirect speech.

3  would,       could,      etc:  no   change INDIRECT: He said he had arrived late because he had lost the address.

I arrived late because I had lost the address.

Past perfect tenses do not change.

They said they might come back early.

We may come back early.

Poor chap - he thought he could fly.

They said that the exam would be difficult.

The exam will be difficult.

I found out that Anna had grown up in Kenya.

Anna grew up in Kenya.

past -> past perfect INDIRECT: She told me that it had been a wonderful trip.

This has been a wonderful trip.

present perfect -> past perfect INDIRECT: I knew my English was getting better.

My English is getting better.

present progressive -> past progressive INDIRECT: She thought she needed help.

I need help.

simple present -> simple past 2 examples       of  natural      tense     changes      after    past    reporting       verbs same tense as the other two: the one that is natural.

The  second sentence is an example of indirect speech, but it uses exactly the James wanted to go home because he didn't like the party.

James didn't like the party. James said he didn't like the party.

usually just the tenses that are natural for the situation. Compare: like the party yesterday and goes home'. The  tenses used in indirect speech are the party', just as it would be strange for Daniel to say, on Sunday,  'James doesn't It would be strange for Daniel to say on Sunday 'James said that he doesn't like grammar •  260  indirect speech: questions and answers They haven't told me where I'm going to work.

She explained what the problem was.

Nobody told me why I had to sign the paper.

I knew how they felt, (     ... how did they feel) The  same structure is used for reporting the answers to questions.

(    Where-tverestaying-...) INDIRECT: I asked where the President and his wife were staying.
